title: cyanea habenata
intro: Cyanea habenata, commonly known as the Hawaiian lobelia or sea rose, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include a range of health benefits such as reducing inflammation, acting as an antioxidant, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Cyanea habenata include its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer agent. The bioactive constituents of the plant include alkaloids and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic actions and health benefits.
side_effects: Cyanea habenata can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and vomiting, due to its toxic compounds. In severe cases, it can cause kidney damage, cardiovascular problems, and even respiratory failure. The improper use of this plant can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and rashes. Furthermore, consuming large amounts of Cyanea habenata can cause neurological symptoms like headaches, dizziness, and tremors.
